mnemonics.title: Event Listener Methods
mnemonics.content:
Click = onClick(View v)
Touch = onTouch(View v, MotionEvent e)
LongClick = onLongClick(View v)
Key = onKey(View v, int keyCode, KeyEvent e)
Focus = onFocusChange(View v, boolean hasFocus)

mnemonics.title: MotionEvent Actions
mnemonics.content:
DOWN - Touch begins
MOVE - Finger moves
UP - Touch ends
CANCEL - System takes over
Remember: D-M-U-C is like 'Don't Use Cr' (start of Cancel)

acronyms.expansion: MotionEvent contains: Action, X, Y, Pressure, Size, Time, Pointer ID
acronyms.usage: Used in OnTouchListener to get touch details

acronyms.expansion: GestureDetector detects: Tap, DoubleTap, LongPress, Scroll, Fling, Pinch

visualTips.visualization:
View Hierarchy: Parent → Child → GrandChild

Touch starts → Dispatch to child → Child.onTouch (if set) → Child.onTouchEvent → If false, bubble to parent → Parent.onTouchEvent → Eventually Activity.onTouchEvent

Think of water bubbling UP from deepest view.

[View]
 |
 +-- OnTouchListener (ALL touch events)
 | ↓ returns true = consumed
 |
 +-- OnClickListener (only tap completed)
 (fires after ACTION_UP if not consumed)

analogies.analogy: Event Listener is like a RECEPTIONIST who receives the phone call (user interaction). Event Handler is like the PERSON who actually takes action and solves the problem.

Think of bubbles rising from the bottom of a drink. The deepest bubble (target view) starts, then bubbles up to the surface (root view). If deeper bubbles don't handle it, the upper ones get a chance.
